message:
feat(abi): implement recursive/errorcheck mutexes, fix barrier race, and harden pthread subsystem

Mutex type support (PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE, PTHREAD_MUTEX_ERRORCHECK):
- Add mutex type constants and storage layout at fixed offsets within
  the opaque pthread_mutex_t: [lock_word(4)][magic(4)][type(4)]
  [owner_tid(4)][lock_count(4)]. These offsets are chosen to maintain
  alignment and fit within the guaranteed 40-byte minimum size.
- pthread_mutex_init now reads the type from the mutexattr (offset 0)
  and stores it, initializes owner_tid to MUTEX_NO_OWNER, and zeroes
  the lock count.
- pthread_mutex_lock dispatches on mutex type: RECURSIVE tracks
  owner_tid and increments lock_count on re-entry (returning EAGAIN on
  u32 overflow); ERRORCHECK returns EDEADLK on re-lock by owner.
- pthread_mutex_trylock follows the same dispatch for ownership tracking.
- pthread_mutex_unlock verifies ownership for RECURSIVE and ERRORCHECK
  (returning EPERM on non-owner unlock), and only fully releases the
  underlying futex when the recursive count reaches zero.
- pthread_mutex_destroy clears extended fields for hygiene.
- pthread_cond_wait and pthread_cond_timedwait save and restore the
  recursive lock count and ownership across the wait, so a thread that
  held a recursive mutex N times will re-acquire it N times after waking.

Barrier race condition fix:
- Pack phase and arrived counter into a single AtomicU64 (phase in upper
  32 bits, arrived in lower 32) so the last arriving thread can advance
  the phase and reset the counter atomically via CAS. Previously these
  were separate AtomicU32 values, and a new thread could arrive between
  the counter reset and phase advance, increment the reset counter, read
  the stale phase, and exit prematurely.
- Add a separate futex_phase AtomicU32 for the futex notification channel
  since futex operates on 32-bit words.
- barrier_destroy now returns EBUSY if threads are currently blocked.

pthread_once fix:
- Restructure the state machine as a retry loop instead of a match on
  compare_exchange failure. The previous code could miss the ONCE_DONE
  transition if the CAS failed due to a concurrent ONCE_IN_PROGRESS->
  ONCE_INIT reset (from a panicking init_routine), causing the waiter
  to spin indefinitely on a state that had already been reset.

Thread creation improvements:
- Accept pthread_attr_t in native_pthread_create: extract stack_size and
  detach_state from either managed attr data or host libc attr, and pass
  stack_size through to core create_thread.
- If created with PTHREAD_CREATE_DETACHED, detach immediately after
  creation and remove from the handle registry to prevent dangling
  pointer access after the thread self-cleans on exit.

TLS key destructor type alignment:
- Stop transmuting the C destructor fn(*mut c_void) to fn(u64) and pass
  it directly to the core, which now accepts the POSIX type natively.

pthread_{set,get}name_np:
- Return ENOSYS when the target thread is not the calling thread, since
  prctl(PR_SET_NAME) only operates on the caller. Previously these
  silently applied the name to the wrong thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
